title: ZooKeeper Client Session Expiry Events
dataSources: ZooKeeper server log lines reporting expired sessions (`Expiring session`, `Session establishment`)
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder on ZooKeeper nodes
spl:
index=zookeeper sourcetype="zookeeper:log"
| search ("Expiring session" OR "expired session" OR "Session.*expired")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as expired_sessions by host, _time
| where expired_sessions > 5
description: Bursts of session expirations indicate GC pauses, network partitions, or mis-tuned session timeouts on clients such as Kafka brokers or HBase region servers.
value: Predicts cascading leader elections and dependent service outages by surfacing ensemble health from the authoritative server log.
implementation: Ensure ZooKeeper logs include session IDs. Deduplicate INFO vs WARN levels. Baseline per environment—Kafka clusters may spike during broker restarts. Correlate with JVM GC logs on ZooKeeper peers.
visualization: Timeline (expired sessions), Table (host, count), Single value (15m rate).
